client_input.title: Anjum Kadari & Anr. vs Union of India & Ors.
client_input.description: The Uttar Pradesh Board of Madarsa Education Act, 2004 has been held unconstitutional by the High Court of Judicature at Allahabad on the grounds that it violates the principle of secularism and Articles 14 and 21A of the Constitution.

citations_found.case_title: Anjum Kadari vs Union Of India on 5 November, 2024

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Anjum Kadari vs Union Of India was about the validity of the Madarsa Act, a regulatory legislation for madarsas (Islamic schools) in the state, which was challenged by several petitioners. The court, led by Justice Dhananjaya Y Chandrachud, ultimately decided that the Madarsa Act is a regulatory legislation that does not violate the basic structure of the Constitution, including the right to education under Article 21-A and the right to manage educational institutions under Article 30.

Judgment Reserved on - 08.02.2024 Judgment delivered on - 22.03.2024 Court No. - 2 Case :- WRIT - C No. - 6049 of 2023 Petitioner :- Anshuman Singh Rathore Respondent :- Union Of India Thr...
citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in this case revolves around the Madarsa Act and its regulations in the state of Uttar Pradesh, with the petitioner Anshuman Singh Rathore challenging the Act's provisions as violative of secularism, the right to education, and the conflict between the Madarsa Act and the UGC Act. The Allahabad High Court, in a judgment delivered on March 22, 2024, decided to uphold the Madarsa Act and its regulations, rejecting the petitioner's grounds of challenge. The court's decision is based on the historical context of madarsas in the state and the provisions of the Madarsa Act and Regulations.
